subject: Dynamic preload docker images script

Guys, post test results and we can get this in  - will run it myself

Patch Set 1:

I think the following line should be changed to avoid an edge case where the script is named something like docker_prepull.sh (or the default name), and the script never exits (stays in the sleep loop).

This change worked for me...
replace: 
while [  $(ps -ef | grep docker | grep pull | wc -l) -gt 0 ]; do

with:
# filter out anything that has the script name
while [  $(ps -ef | grep docker | grep pull | grep -v $0 | wc -l) -gt 0 ]; do

Patch Set 1:

Another issue we saw when we did a prepull to file output is that there are repeated entries, so we need to do a "uniq". For remote connectivity over inter-continental WAN, docker pulls can take a long time (e.g. checking whether the image is up-to date seemed to be costly).

Patch Set 7:

Hi Alexis,

Bellow are the configuration options:


-h -> help menu

-u -> login user name
[Default : docker] 

-p -> login password
[Default : docker]


-l -> path for scanning directory for values.yaml files
[Default : current directory]

-r -> repository name/ url
[Default : nexus repo]

Thanks
